name: Abenaki
description: The Abenaki people are indigenous to Vermont, New Hampshire, Maine, and southern Quebec.

name: Abkhaz
description: The Abkhaz are indigenous to Abkhazia on the eastern coast of the Black Sea.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Anangu
description: The Anangu are indigenous to the Uluru-Kata Tjuta area of central Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Arrernte
description: The Arrernte are indigenous to the area around Alice Springs in central Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Bundjalung
description: The Bundjalung are indigenous to the northern coast of New South Wales, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Dja Dja Wurrung
description: The Dja Dja Wurrung are indigenous to the goldfields region of central Victoria, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Gadigal
description: The Gadigal are indigenous to the area of present-day Sydney, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Gunditjmara
description: The Gunditjmara are indigenous to western Victoria, known for the Budj Bim aquaculture system.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Jawoyn
description: The Jawoyn are indigenous to the Katherine region of the Northern Territory, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Kamilaroi
description: The Kamilaroi (Gamilaraay) are indigenous to northeastern New South Wales.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Kaurna
description: The Kaurna are indigenous to the Adelaide Plains of South Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Larrakia
description: The Larrakia are indigenous to the Darwin area of the Northern Territory, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Luritja
description: The Luritja are indigenous to the area west of Alice Springs in central Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Martu
description: The Martu are indigenous to the western desert region of Western Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Narungga
description: The Narungga are indigenous to Yorke Peninsula in South Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Noongar
description: The Noongar are indigenous to the southwest corner of Western Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Nyungar
description: The Nyungar people are indigenous to the southern coast of Western Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Palawa
description: The Palawa are the indigenous people of Tasmania (lutruwita).

name: Aboriginal Australians - Pitjantjatjara
description: The Pitjantjatjara are indigenous to the Western Desert region of central Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Tiwi
description: The Tiwi are indigenous to the Tiwi Islands north of Darwin, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Warlpiri
description: The Warlpiri are indigenous to the Tanami Desert of central Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Wiradjuri
description: The Wiradjuri are indigenous to central New South Wales, the largest Aboriginal group in the state.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Wurundjeri
description: The Wurundjeri are indigenous to the area of present-day Melbourne, Australia.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Yolngu
description: The Yolngu are indigenous to Arnhem Land in northern Australia, known for their didgeridoo tradition.

name: Aboriginal Australians - Yorta Yorta
description: The Yorta Yorta are indigenous to the Murray-Goulburn Rivers area of Victoria, Australia.

name: Aché
description: The Aché people are indigenous to the eastern forests of Paraguay.

name: Adivasi - Bhil
description: The Bhil are one of the largest Adivasi groups in western India.

name: Adivasi - Bodo
description: The Bodo are indigenous to Assam in northeastern India.

name: Adivasi - Chenchu
description: The Chenchu are indigenous hunter-gatherers of Andhra Pradesh, India.

name: Adivasi - Gond
description: The Gond are one of the largest Adivasi (tribal) groups in central India.

name: Adivasi - Ho
description: The Ho people are indigenous to the Singhbhum district of Jharkhand, India.

name: Adivasi - Khasi
description: The Khasi are indigenous to the Khasi Hills of Meghalaya, northeastern India, known for their matrilineal society.

name: Adivasi - Manipuri
description: The Manipuri (Meitei) are indigenous to Manipur in northeastern India.

name: Adivasi - Mizo
description: The Mizo people are indigenous to Mizoram in northeastern India.

name: Adivasi - Munda
description: The Munda are indigenous to the Chota Nagpur Plateau of eastern India.

name: Adivasi - Naga
description: The Naga peoples are indigenous to Nagaland and Manipur in northeastern India and northwestern Myanmar.

name: Adivasi - Oraon
description: The Oraon are indigenous to the Chota Nagpur Plateau of eastern India.

name: Adivasi - Santhal
description: The Santhal are one of the largest indigenous groups in India, in Jharkhand and West Bengal.

name: Adivasi - Tripuri
description: The Tripuri (Tipra) are indigenous to Tripura in northeastern India.

name: Adivasi - Warli
description: The Warli are indigenous to Maharashtra, India, known for their distinctive painting tradition.

name: Afar
description: The Afar people are indigenous to the Danakil Desert region of Ethiopia, Eritrea, and Djibouti.

name: Ainu
description: The Ainu are the indigenous people of Hokkaido, Japan, and the Kuril Islands.

name: Akha
description: The Akha people are indigenous to the highlands of Myanmar, Laos, Thailand, and Yunnan, China.

name: Akimel O'odham
description: The Akimel O'odham (Pima) are indigenous to central and southern Arizona along the Gila River.

name: Aleut
description: The Aleut (Unangan) are indigenous to the Aleutian Islands and western Alaska.

name: Algonquin
description: The Algonquin people are indigenous to the Ottawa River valley in Ontario and Quebec.

name: Altai
description: The Altai people are indigenous to the Altai Mountains of southern Siberia.

name: Amazigh
description: The Amazigh (Berber) peoples are indigenous to North Africa, from Morocco to Libya.

name: Andamanese
description: The Andamanese peoples are indigenous to the Andaman Islands in the Bay of Bengal.

name: Anishinaabe
description: The Anishinaabe encompasses the Ojibwe, Odawa, and Potawatomi peoples of the Great Lakes and Ontario.

name: Apache
description: The Apache are indigenous to the American Southwest, known for leaders like Geronimo and Cochise.

name: Apalachee
description: The Apalachee people were indigenous to the Florida panhandle and southern Georgia.

name: Arapaho
description: The Arapaho people are indigenous to the central Great Plains, from Wyoming to Colorado.

name: Arhuaco
description: The Arhuaco (Ika) people are indigenous to the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta in Colombia.

name: Arikara
description: The Arikara people are indigenous to the Missouri River area of the Dakotas, known as skilled farmers.

name: Asháninka
description: The Asháninka are one of the largest indigenous groups in the Peruvian Amazon.

name: Assiniboine
description: The Assiniboine (Nakoda) people are indigenous to the northern Great Plains of Montana and Saskatchewan.

name: Atakapa
description: The Atakapa people are indigenous to the Gulf Coast of southwestern Louisiana and southeastern Texas.

name: Atikamekw
description: The Atikamekw people are indigenous to the upper St. Maurice River area in Quebec.

name: Avar
description: The Avars are indigenous to Dagestan in the North Caucasus.

name: Awá-Guajá
description: The Awá-Guajá are one of the most endangered uncontacted peoples, indigenous to Maranhão, Brazil.

name: Aymara
description: The Aymara people are indigenous to the Altiplano of the Andes in Bolivia and Peru.

name: Bad River Ojibwe
description: The Bad River Band of the Lake Superior Chippewa are in northern Wisconsin.

name: Baloch
description: The Baloch people are indigenous to Balochistan, spanning Pakistan, Iran, and Afghanistan.

name: Bannock
description: The Bannock people are indigenous to the Snake River Plain of southeastern Idaho.

name: Basque
description: The Basque people are indigenous to the Basque Country spanning Spain and France, with one of Europe's oldest cultures.

name: Batwa
description: The Batwa (Twa) are indigenous to the forests around the Great Lakes region of Central Africa.

name: Beothuk
description: The Beothuk were the indigenous people of Newfoundland. Tragically, the Beothuk are considered extinct as a distinct people.

name: Berber - Kabyle
description: The Kabyle are an Amazigh people indigenous to the Kabylie region of northern Algeria.

name: Berber - Rif
description: The Riffians are an Amazigh people indigenous to the Rif Mountains of northern Morocco.

name: Berber - Siwi
description: The Siwi are Berber people indigenous to the Siwa Oasis in western Egypt.

name: Berber - Tuareg Kel Ahaggar
description: The Kel Ahaggar are a Tuareg confederation in the Hoggar Mountains of southern Algeria.

name: Berber - Tuareg Kel Air
description: The Kel Aïr are a Tuareg confederation in the Aïr Mountains of Niger.

name: Blackfoot (Siksika)
description: The Siksika (Blackfoot proper) are indigenous to southern Alberta, part of the Blackfoot Confederacy.

name: Blackfoot
description: The Blackfoot Confederacy includes the Siksika, Kainai, and Piikani peoples of the northern Great Plains.

name: Borana
description: The Borana Oromo are indigenous pastoralists in southern Ethiopia and northern Kenya.

name: Bribri
description: The Bribri people are indigenous to the Talamanca region of Costa Rica.

name: Burns Paiute
description: The Burns Paiute Tribe are Northern Paiute in east-central Oregon.

name: Buryat
description: The Buryat are indigenous to the area around Lake Baikal in Siberia.

name: Caddo
description: The Caddo are indigenous to the area where Texas, Louisiana, Arkansas, and Oklahoma meet, known as mound builders.

name: Cahuilla
description: The Cahuilla people are indigenous to the inland areas of southern California, including the Coachella Valley.

name: Calusa
description: The Calusa were indigenous to the southwest coast of Florida, known as powerful maritime people.

name: Carrier
description: The Carrier (Dakelh) people are indigenous to the central interior of British Columbia.

name: Catawba
description: The Catawba people are indigenous to the Catawba River valley of the Carolinas.

name: Cayuga
description: The Cayuga are one of the six nations of the Haudenosaunee Confederacy, around Cayuga Lake.

name: Cayuse
description: The Cayuse people are indigenous to the Blue Mountains area of Oregon and Washington.

name: Chamorro
description: The Chamorro are indigenous to the Mariana Islands, including Guam.

name: Chechen
description: The Chechens are indigenous to the Chechen Republic in the North Caucasus.

name: Chehalis
description: The Confederated Tribes of the Chehalis Reservation are in southwestern Washington.

name: Cherokee
description: The Cherokee are one of the largest indigenous nations in the US, originally from the southeastern woodlands. Forced removal on the Trail of Tears in 1838.

name: Cheyenne River Sioux
description: The Cheyenne River Sioux are a Lakota band in central South Dakota.

name: Chickasaw
description: The Chickasaw are indigenous to the southeastern US, in present-day Mississippi, Alabama, and Tennessee.

name: Chin
description: The Chin people are indigenous to Chin State in western Myanmar.

name: Chinantec
description: The Chinantec people are indigenous to the Chinantla region of Oaxaca, Mexico.

name: Chinook
description: The Chinook people are indigenous to the lower Columbia River and Pacific coast of present-day Washington and Oregon.

name: Chippewa-Cree
description: The Chippewa-Cree are indigenous to the Rocky Boy's Reservation in north-central Montana.

name: Chiricahua Apache
description: The Chiricahua Apache are indigenous to southeastern Arizona and southwestern New Mexico, known for Geronimo.

name: Chitimacha
description: The Chitimacha people are indigenous to the area around the Atchafalaya Basin in Louisiana.

name: Choctaw
description: The Choctaw are indigenous to the southeastern US, the first of the Five Civilized Tribes removed via the Trail of Tears.

name: Ch'ol
description: The Ch'ol are a Maya people indigenous to northern Chiapas, Mexico.

name: Chukchi
description: The Chukchi are indigenous to the Chukchi Peninsula of northeastern Siberia.

name: Chumash
description: The Chumash are indigenous to the southern California coast, known for their plank canoes (tomols).

name: Circassian
description: The Circassians (Adyghe) are indigenous to the northwestern Caucasus region.

name: Coast Salish
description: The Coast Salish peoples are indigenous to the coastal areas of British Columbia and Washington State.

name: Cocopah
description: The Cocopah people are indigenous to the Colorado River delta area in southwestern Arizona.

name: Coeur d'Alene
description: The Coeur d'Alene people are indigenous to the area around Coeur d'Alene Lake in northern Idaho.

name: Colville
description: The Confederated Tribes of the Colville Reservation include 12 tribes in northeastern Washington.

name: Comanche
description: The Comanche are indigenous to the southern Great Plains, known as exceptional horsemen and the dominant force on the southern plains.

name: Coos-Lower Umpqua-Siuslaw
description: The Confederated Tribes include three tribes from the Oregon coast.

name: Coos
description: The Coos people are indigenous to the area around Coos Bay on the southern Oregon coast.

name: Coquille
description: The Coquille people are indigenous to the Coquille River valley on the southern Oregon coast.

name: Coushatta
description: The Coushatta (Koasati) people are indigenous to the southeastern US, now in Louisiana and Texas.

name: Cow Creek Umpqua
description: The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua are indigenous to the South Umpqua Valley in Oregon.

name: Cowlitz
description: The Cowlitz Indian Tribe is indigenous to southwestern Washington.

name: Cree
description: The Cree are one of the largest Indigenous groups in Canada, spanning from Alberta to Quebec.

name: Crimean Tatar
description: The Crimean Tatars are indigenous to the Crimean Peninsula.

name: Crow
description: The Crow (Apsáalooke) people are indigenous to the Yellowstone River area of Montana.

name: Dai
description: The Dai people are indigenous to Yunnan province in southwestern China, related to Thai peoples.

name: Dakota
description: The Dakota (Santee Sioux) are indigenous to the Minnesota and eastern Dakotas region.

name: Datoga
description: The Datoga are pastoralist people indigenous to north-central Tanzania.

name: Dayak
description: The Dayak are the indigenous peoples of Borneo (Kalimantan), including many distinct groups.

name: Dene
description: The Dene people are indigenous to the subarctic regions of Canada's Northwest Territories.

name: Diaguita
description: The Diaguita are indigenous to the northwestern Argentine and Chilean Andes.

name: Dinka
description: The Dinka are the largest ethnic group in South Sudan, indigenous to the Nile Basin.

name: Dogon
description: The Dogon are indigenous to the Bandiagara Escarpment in Mali, known for their astronomy and mask traditions.

name: Duwamish

name: Eastern Shoshone
description: The Eastern Shoshone are indigenous to the Wind River area of Wyoming.

name: Emberá
description: The Emberá people are indigenous to the Pacific coast of Colombia and Panama.

name: Esselen
description: The Esselen people are indigenous to the Big Sur coast and inland valleys of the central California coast.

name: Evenki
description: The Evenki are indigenous to a vast area of Siberia, from the Yenisei River to the Pacific.

name: Fijian
description: The iTaukei are the indigenous people of Fiji.

name: Flathead
description: The Flathead (Salish-Kootenai) people are indigenous to western Montana.

name: Forest County Potawatomi
description: The Forest County Potawatomi Community is in northeastern Wisconsin.

name: Fort Sill Apache
description: The Fort Sill Apache are Chiricahua Apache historically from the Southwest, now in Oklahoma.

name: Fulani
description: The Fulani are one of the largest nomadic groups worldwide, spread across West and Central Africa.

name: Fur
description: The Fur people are indigenous to Darfur in western Sudan.

name: Gagauz
description: The Gagauz are a Turkic people indigenous to Moldova and surrounding areas.

name: Garifuna
description: The Garifuna are an Afro-Indigenous people along the Caribbean coast of Central America.

name: Gitxsan
description: The Gitxsan people are indigenous to the upper Skeena River area of British Columbia.

name: Goshute
description: The Goshute people are indigenous to the desert regions of western Utah and eastern Nevada.

name: Grande Ronde
description: The Confederated Tribes of Grand Ronde include Kalapuya, Molalla, Clackamas, and other peoples of western Oregon.

name: Gros Ventre
description: The Gros Ventre (Aaniiih) people are indigenous to the north-central Montana plains.

name: Guaraní

name: Hadza
description: The Hadza are one of the last remaining hunter-gatherer peoples in Africa, indigenous to Tanzania.

name: Haida

name: Hamer
description: The Hamer people are indigenous to the Omo Valley of southwestern Ethiopia.

name: Haudenosaunee

name: Havasupai
description: The Havasupai people are indigenous to the Grand Canyon, known for Havasu Falls.

name: Herero
description: The Herero people are indigenous to Namibia and Botswana.

name: Hidatsa
description: The Hidatsa people are indigenous to the Knife River area of North Dakota, closely related to the Mandan.

name: Himba
description: The Himba are indigenous to the Kunene region of Namibia, known for their ochre body paint.

name: Hmong
description: The Hmong are indigenous to the mountainous regions of southern China, Vietnam, Laos, and Thailand.

name: Ho-Chunk
description: The Ho-Chunk (Winnebago) people are indigenous to the area of present-day Wisconsin and Illinois.

name: Hoopa
description: The Hoopa Valley Tribe is indigenous to the Trinity River area in northwestern California.

name: Hopi
description: The Hopi people are indigenous to northeastern Arizona, living in some of the oldest continuously inhabited villages in North America.

name: Hualapai
description: The Hualapai people are indigenous to the Grand Canyon area of northwestern Arizona.

name: Huichol
description: The Huichol (Wixárika) people are indigenous to the Sierra Madre Occidental of western Mexico.

name: Igorot
description: The Igorot are indigenous to the Cordillera mountains of northern Luzon, Philippines.

name: Illinois
description: The Illinois Confederacy (Illiniwek) were indigenous to the area of present-day Illinois and surrounding states.

name: Ingush
description: The Ingush are indigenous to the Ingush Republic in the North Caucasus.

name: Innu
description: The Innu are indigenous to the interior of Labrador and eastern Quebec.

name: Inuit Nunangat
description: The Inuit are indigenous to the Arctic regions of Canada, known for their adaptation to extreme environments.

name: Inuvialuit
description: The Inuvialuit are indigenous to the western Canadian Arctic.

name: Iowa

name: Iraqw
description: The Iraqw are indigenous to the Mbulu Highlands of Tanzania.

name: Itelmens
description: The Itelmen are indigenous to the Kamchatka Peninsula of the Russian Far East.

name: Jarawa
description: The Jarawa are one of the most isolated indigenous peoples, on the Andaman Islands.

name: Jicarilla Apache
description: The Jicarilla Apache are indigenous to the area of northern New Mexico and southern Colorado.

name: Jumma
description: The Jumma are indigenous to the Chittagong Hill Tracts of Bangladesh.

name: Kachin
description: The Kachin (Jingpo) are indigenous to Kachin State in northern Myanmar.

name: Kainai
description: The Kainai (Blood) are part of the Blackfoot Confederacy, with a large reserve in southern Alberta.

name: Kaingang
description: The Kaingang are indigenous to southern Brazil, one of the most populous Indigenous groups in the country.

name: Kalapuya
description: The Kalapuya people are indigenous to the Willamette Valley of western Oregon.

name: Kalinago
description: The Kalinago (Carib) are indigenous to the Lesser Antilles, particularly Dominica.

name: Kanaky
description: The Kanak are the indigenous Melanesian people of New Caledonia (Kanaky).

name: Kaqchikel
description: The Kaqchikel are a Maya people indigenous to the central highlands of Guatemala.

name: Karelian
description: The Karelians are a Finnic people indigenous to Karelia, spanning Finland and Russia.

name: Karen
description: The Karen people are indigenous to the Thailand-Myanmar border region.

name: Karuk
description: The Karuk people are indigenous to the middle Klamath River area in northwestern California.

name: Kaw
description: The Kaw (Kansa) people are indigenous to the Kansas River area, the state of Kansas was named after them.

name: Kayapó
description: The Kayapó people are indigenous to the Brazilian Amazon, known for their environmental activism.

name: Ket
description: The Ket are a small indigenous group on the Yenisei River, with an endangered isolated language.

name: Khanty
description: The Khanty people are indigenous to the Ob River basin of western Siberia.

name: Khoi
description: The Khoikhoi (Khoi) are indigenous to southwestern Africa, historically pastoralists.

name: K'iche'
description: The K'iche' are the largest Maya group in Guatemala, authors of the Popol Vuh.

name: Kichwa
description: The Kichwa people are indigenous to the highlands and Amazon of Ecuador.

name: Kickapoo
description: The Kickapoo people are indigenous to the area of present-day Wisconsin and Illinois.

name: Kiowa
description: The Kiowa people are indigenous to the southern Great Plains, known for their rich artistic traditions.

name: Klamath
description: The Klamath Tribes are indigenous to southern Oregon and northern California around Upper Klamath Lake.

name: Kogi
description: The Kogi people are indigenous to the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta in Colombia, known as guardians of the Earth.

name: Komi
description: The Komi people are indigenous to the Komi Republic in northeastern European Russia.

name: Koryak
description: The Koryak are indigenous to the northern Kamchatka Peninsula of the Russian Far East.

name: Ktunaxa
description: The Ktunaxa (Kootenay) people are indigenous to southeastern British Columbia and neighboring areas.

name: Kumeyaay
description: The Kumeyaay people are indigenous to the San Diego area and northern Baja California.

name: Kuna
description: The Kuna (Guna) people are indigenous to the San Blas Islands and eastern Panama.

name: Kurds
description: The Kurdish people are indigenous to Kurdistan, spanning parts of Turkey, Iraq, Iran, and Syria.

name: Kwakwaka'wakw
description: The Kwakwaka'wakw are indigenous to northern Vancouver Island and the adjacent mainland coast of BC.

name: Lac du Flambeau Ojibwe
description: The Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa are in northern Wisconsin.

name: Lahu
description: The Lahu are indigenous to the mountainous regions of Myanmar, China, Laos, and Thailand.

name: Lakota
description: The Lakota (Teton Sioux) are the westernmost of the Sioux peoples, known for leaders like Sitting Bull and Crazy Horse.

name: Leech Lake Ojibwe
description: The Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe are in north-central Minnesota.

name: Lenape
description: The Lenape (Delaware) are indigenous to the area of present-day New Jersey, eastern Pennsylvania, and Delaware.

name: Lenca
description: The Lenca people are indigenous to Honduras and El Salvador.

name: Lezgin
description: The Lezgins are indigenous to southern Dagestan and northern Azerbaijan.

name: Lipan Apache
description: The Lipan Apache are indigenous to the Edwards Plateau of central Texas.

name: Lisu
description: The Lisu people are indigenous to the Nujiang River area of Yunnan, China, and surrounding countries.

name: Livonian
description: The Livonians are indigenous to the northern coast of Latvia, with a critically endangered language.

name: Lobi
description: The Lobi people are indigenous to the border region of Burkina Faso, Ivory Coast, and Ghana.

name: Lokono
description: The Lokono (Arawak) are indigenous to the coast of the Guianas.

name: Luiseno
description: The Luiseño people are indigenous to the area of present-day northern San Diego County, California.

name: Lumad
description: The Lumad are the non-Moro indigenous peoples of Mindanao, Philippines.

name: Lummi
description: The Lummi Nation are indigenous to the area around Bellingham Bay in Washington State.

name: Māori - Ngāti Porou
description: Ngāti Porou is a major Māori iwi on the East Coast of the North Island.

name: Māori - Ngāti Tūwharetoa
description: Ngāti Tūwharetoa is a Māori iwi in the Taupo region of the central North Island.

name: Māori - Te Arawa
description: Te Arawa is a confederation of Māori iwi in the Rotorua and Bay of Plenty region.

name: Māori
description: The Māori are the indigenous Polynesian people of New Zealand (Aotearoa).

name: Métis
description: The Métis are a distinct Indigenous people with mixed First Nations and European ancestry, centered in the Red River area.

name: Maasai
description: The Maasai are indigenous to southern Kenya and northern Tanzania, known for their pastoralist culture.

name: Macushi
description: The Macushi are indigenous to the savannas of Guyana and Brazil.

name: Maidu
description: The Maidu people are indigenous to the Sacramento Valley and Sierra Nevada foothills of northeastern California.

name: Makah
description: The Makah people are indigenous to the northwestern tip of the Olympic Peninsula, known for whaling.

name: Makuxi
description: The Makuxi are indigenous to the borderlands of Brazil, Guyana, and Venezuela.

name: Maliseet
description: The Maliseet (Wolastoqiyik) people are indigenous to the Saint John River valley in New Brunswick.

name: Mam
description: The Mam are a Maya people indigenous to the western highlands of Guatemala.

name: Mandan
description: The Mandan people are indigenous to the upper Missouri River area of North Dakota, known as skilled farmers.

name: Mansi
description: The Mansi are indigenous to the eastern slopes of the Ural Mountains in Russia.

name: Mapuche
description: The Mapuche are indigenous to south-central Chile and Argentina, known for their centuries-long resistance.

name: Maroon - Saramaka
description: The Saramaka are Maroon people indigenous to the interior of Suriname.

name: Matsés
description: The Matsés people are indigenous to the border region between Peru and Brazil in the Amazon.

name: Maya
description: The Maya people are indigenous to southern Mexico, Guatemala, Belize, and Honduras, known for their advanced civilization.

name: Mayo
description: The Mayo (Yoreme) people are indigenous to Sinaloa and Sonora in northwestern Mexico.

name: Mazatec
description: The Mazatec people are indigenous to northern Oaxaca, Mexico.

name: Menominee
description: The Menominee people are indigenous to the area of present-day Wisconsin and upper Michigan.

name: Mescalero Apache
description: The Mescalero Apache are indigenous to the Sacramento Mountains of southern New Mexico.

name: Meskwaki
description: The Meskwaki (Fox) people are indigenous to the upper Mississippi Valley area of Wisconsin and Iowa.

name: Miami
description: The Miami people are indigenous to the area of present-day Indiana and Ohio.

name: Miao
description: The Miao (including Hmong subgroups) are indigenous to southern China and Southeast Asia.

name: Miccosukee
description: The Miccosukee people are indigenous to the Florida Everglades.

name: Mi'kmaq
description: The Mi'kmaq are indigenous to the Atlantic provinces of Canada and parts of Maine.

name: Mille Lacs Ojibwe
description: The Mille Lacs Band of Ojibwe are in central Minnesota.

name: Miskito
description: The Miskito people are indigenous to the Mosquito Coast of Honduras and Nicaragua.

name: Miwok
description: The Miwok people are indigenous to central California, from the Sierra Nevada foothills to the coast.

name: Mixtec
description: The Mixtec people are indigenous to western Oaxaca and neighboring states in southern Mexico.

name: Modoc
description: The Modoc people are indigenous to the area around Tule Lake and the Lost River in southern Oregon and northern California.

name: Mohave-Apache
description: The Yavapai-Apache Nation is in the Verde Valley of Arizona.

name: Mohawk of Kahnawake
description: The Mohawk community of Kahnawake is located on the south shore of the St. Lawrence River near Montreal.

name: Mohawk
description: The Mohawk are the easternmost nation of the Haudenosaunee Confederacy, Keepers of the Eastern Door.

name: Mohegan
description: The Mohegan people are indigenous to the Thames River area of southeastern Connecticut.

name: Mojave
description: The Mojave people are indigenous to the Mojave Desert along the Colorado River.

name: Mon
description: The Mon people are indigenous to the Mon State of southern Myanmar and parts of Thailand.

name: Mongolian
description: The Mongolian people are indigenous to the steppes of Central and East Asia.

name: Muckleshoot

name: Munduruku
description: The Munduruku are indigenous to the Tapajós River area of the Brazilian Amazon.

name: Mursi
description: The Mursi are indigenous to the Omo Valley of southwestern Ethiopia, known for their lip plates.

name: Muscogee
description: The Muscogee (Creek) are indigenous to the southeastern US, known for the Creek Confederacy.

name: Nahua
description: The Nahua are the largest Indigenous group in Mexico, descendants of the Aztec Empire.

name: Nakota
description: The Nakota (Yankton Sioux) are indigenous to the central Dakotas and southern Manitoba.

name: Nama
description: The Nama are indigenous to Namibia and South Africa, the largest group of the Khoikhoi.

name: Nanai
description: The Nanai are indigenous to the Amur River basin in the Russian Far East and northeastern China.

name: Narragansett
description: The Narragansett people are indigenous to present-day Rhode Island.

name: Nasa
description: The Nasa (Páez) people are indigenous to the southwestern highlands of Colombia.

name: Naskapi
description: The Naskapi are indigenous to the Labrador Peninsula of eastern Canada, known as caribou hunters.

name: Natchez
description: The Natchez people are indigenous to the lower Mississippi River area, known for their complex chiefdom.

name: Native Hawaiian
description: Native Hawaiians are the indigenous Polynesian people of the Hawaiian Islands.

name: Navajo
description: The Navajo (Diné) are the largest federally recognized tribe in the US, indigenous to the Four Corners region.

name: Ndebele
description: The Ndebele people are indigenous to the Limpopo and Mpumalanga provinces of South Africa.

name: Nenets (European)
description: The European Nenets inhabit the tundra of the Nenets Autonomous Okrug in Arctic Russia.

name: Nenets
description: The Nenets are indigenous to the Arctic coast of Russia, known as reindeer herders.

name: Nez Perce
description: The Nez Perce are indigenous to the Columbia River Plateau, known for Chief Joseph's resistance.

name: Ngäbe-Buglé
description: The Ngäbe-Buglé are the largest indigenous group in Panama.

name: Ngāi Tahu
description: Ngāi Tahu is the principal Māori iwi (tribe) of the South Island of New Zealand.

name: Ngāpuhi
description: Ngāpuhi is the largest Māori iwi, indigenous to the Northland region of New Zealand.

name: Nganasan
description: The Nganasan are the northernmost indigenous people of Eurasia, on the Taymyr Peninsula.

name: Nicobarese
description: The Nicobarese are indigenous to the Nicobar Islands in the Bay of Bengal.

name: Nisqually
description: The Nisqually people are indigenous to the Nisqually River watershed in western Washington.

name: Nivkh
description: The Nivkh are indigenous to Sakhalin Island and the Amur River area of the Russian Far East.

name: Nlaka'pamux
description: The Nlaka'pamux (Thompson) people are indigenous to the interior of British Columbia.

name: Nooksack
description: The Nooksack people are indigenous to the Nooksack River valley in northwestern Washington.

name: Northern Cheyenne
description: The Northern Cheyenne are indigenous to the northern Great Plains, now in southeastern Montana.

name: Northern Paiute
description: The Northern Paiute are indigenous to the Great Basin region of Nevada, Oregon, and California.

name: Nuba
description: The Nuba peoples are indigenous to the Nuba Mountains of Sudan.

name: Nuer
description: The Nuer people are indigenous to the Nile Valley in South Sudan and Ethiopia.

name: Nuu-chah-nulth
description: The Nuu-chah-nulth people are indigenous to the west coast of Vancouver Island, known as skilled whalers.

name: Odawa
description: The Odawa (Ottawa) people are indigenous to the northern Great Lakes region, known as skilled traders.

name: Ogiek
description: The Ogiek are indigenous hunter-gatherers of the Mau Forest in Kenya.

name: Ohlone
description: The Ohlone (Costanoan) people are indigenous to the San Francisco Bay Area and central California coast.

name: Ojibwe
description: The Ojibwe (Chippewa/Anishinaabe) are one of the largest Indigenous groups in North America, indigenous to the Great Lakes region.

name: Omaha
description: The Omaha people are indigenous to the area around present-day Omaha, Nebraska.

name: Oneida of Wisconsin
description: The Oneida Nation of Wisconsin relocated from New York in the 1820s.

name: Oneida
description: The Oneida are one of the six nations of the Haudenosaunee Confederacy in present-day New York.

name: Onondaga
description: The Onondaga are the central fire keepers of the Haudenosaunee Confederacy in present-day New York.

name: Orang Asli
description: The Orang Asli are the indigenous people of Peninsular Malaysia.

name: Oromo
description: The Oromo are the largest ethnic group in Ethiopia, indigenous to the central and southern highlands.

name: Osage
description: The Osage Nation is indigenous to the central United States, from Missouri to Oklahoma, known for their wealth from oil.

name: Ossetian
description: The Ossetians are indigenous to the central Caucasus, spanning North and South Ossetia.

name: Otoe-Missouria
description: The Otoe-Missouria are Siouan peoples indigenous to the Missouri River area of Nebraska.

name: Otomí
description: The Otomí are indigenous to the central Mexican highlands.

name: Palouse
description: The Palouse people are indigenous to the Palouse River region of eastern Washington.

name: Papua New Guinean Highlands
description: The Highland peoples of Papua New Guinea represent hundreds of distinct cultures in the mountainous interior.

name: Papuan peoples
description: The Papuan peoples are indigenous to the western half of New Guinea (Papua, Indonesia).

name: Pascua Yaqui
description: The Pascua Yaqui Tribe is near Tucson, Arizona, with roots in Sonora, Mexico.

name: Passamaquoddy
description: The Passamaquoddy people are indigenous to the St. Croix River area at the Maine-New Brunswick border.

name: Pataxó
description: The Pataxó people are indigenous to the Atlantic coast of Bahia, Brazil.

name: Pawnee
description: The Pawnee are indigenous to the central Great Plains of Nebraska and Kansas, known as astronomers and farmers.

name: Pemón
description: The Pemón are indigenous to the Gran Sabana region of southeastern Venezuela.

name: Penobscot
description: The Penobscot people are indigenous to the Penobscot River valley in Maine.

name: Pequot
description: The Pequot people are indigenous to southeastern Connecticut.

name: Piikani
description: The Piikani (Peigan) are part of the Blackfoot Confederacy in southern Alberta.

name: Pine Ridge Oglala
description: The Oglala Lakota at Pine Ridge Reservation are known for the Wounded Knee incident.

name: Pokot
description: The Pokot are indigenous to the border region of Kenya and Uganda.

name: Pomo
description: The Pomo are indigenous to the area north of San Francisco, particularly around Clear Lake and the Russian River.

name: Ponca
description: The Ponca people are indigenous to the confluence of the Niobrara and Missouri Rivers in Nebraska.

name: Potawatomi
description: The Potawatomi people are indigenous to the Great Lakes region, part of the Council of Three Fires with the Ojibwe and Odawa.

name: Powhatan
description: The Powhatan Confederacy was indigenous to the Virginia tidewater area, known for the encounter with Jamestown settlers.

name: Pueblo of Acoma
description: The Pueblo of Acoma, known as Sky City, is one of the oldest continuously inhabited communities in the US.

name: Pueblo of Cochiti
description: Cochiti Pueblo is indigenous to the area along the Rio Grande south of Santa Fe.

name: Pueblo of Isleta
description: Isleta Pueblo is one of the largest of the Rio Grande Pueblos, south of Albuquerque.

name: Pueblo of Jemez
description: Jemez Pueblo is the sole remaining Towa-speaking community in New Mexico.

name: Pueblo of Laguna
description: The Pueblo of Laguna is indigenous to west-central New Mexico.

name: Pueblo of Nambe
description: Nambé Pueblo is a Tewa-speaking community near Santa Fe.

name: Pueblo of Ohkay Owingeh
description: Ohkay Owingeh (San Juan Pueblo) is the site of the first Spanish colonial capital of New Mexico.

name: Pueblo of Picuris
description: Picuris Pueblo is a Tiwa-speaking community in the Sangre de Cristo Mountains.

name: Pueblo of Pojoaque
description: Pojoaque Pueblo is a Tewa-speaking community between Santa Fe and Española.

name: Pueblo of San Felipe
description: San Felipe Pueblo is one of the most traditional of the Rio Grande Pueblos.

name: Pueblo of San Ildefonso
description: San Ildefonso Pueblo is known for its distinctive black-on-black pottery tradition.

name: Pueblo of Sandia
description: Sandia Pueblo is indigenous to the area north of Albuquerque at the base of the Sandia Mountains.

name: Pueblo of Santa Ana
description: Santa Ana Pueblo is along the Jemez River in New Mexico.

name: Pueblo of Santa Clara
description: Santa Clara Pueblo is indigenous to the area near present-day Española, New Mexico.

name: Pueblo of Santo Domingo
description: Santo Domingo (Kewa) Pueblo is one of the largest and most traditional of the Rio Grande Pueblos.

name: Pueblo of Taos
description: Taos Pueblo has been continuously inhabited for over 1,000 years and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Pueblo of Tesuque
description: Tesuque Pueblo is a Tewa-speaking community north of Santa Fe.

name: Pueblo of Zia
description: Zia Pueblo is the source of the sun symbol on New Mexico's state flag.

name: Pueblo of Zuni
description: The Pueblo of Zuni is known for its distinctive Zuni art and jewelry traditions.

name: Purépecha
description: The Purépecha (Tarascan) people are indigenous to Michoacán, western Mexico.

name: Puyallup
description: The Puyallup people have inhabited the area around present-day Tacoma, Washington and the Puyallup River valley.

name: Pygmy - Aka
description: The Aka people are indigenous to the tropical forests of the Central African Republic and Republic of Congo.

name: Pygmy - Baka
description: The Baka people are indigenous to the tropical forests of Cameroon, Republic of Congo, and Gabon.

name: Pygmy - Efe
description: The Efe are indigenous to the Ituri Forest of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

name: Pygmy - Mbuti
description: The Mbuti are indigenous to the Ituri Forest of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

name: Pygmy - Twa (Batwa)
description: The Batwa of Uganda are indigenous to the forests of southwestern Uganda.

name: Q'eqchi'
description: The Q'eqchi' are a Maya people indigenous to the Verapaces region of Guatemala.

name: Quapaw
description: The Quapaw (Ogaxpa) people are indigenous to the Arkansas River valley.

name: Quechan
description: The Quechan (Yuma) people are indigenous to the lower Colorado River area near present-day Yuma, Arizona.

name: Quechua
description: The Quechua are indigenous to the Andes, descendants of the Inca Empire, the most spoken Indigenous language family in the Americas.

name: Quinault
description: The Quinault people are indigenous to the Pacific coast of Washington's Olympic Peninsula.

name: Red Lake Ojibwe
description: The Red Lake Band of Chippewa Indians hold the only closed reservation in Minnesota.

name: Rendille
description: The Rendille are indigenous camel pastoralists in northern Kenya.

name: Rohingya
description: The Rohingya are a Muslim minority in Rakhine State, Myanmar.

name: Romani
description: The Romani people originated in northern India and have been in Europe for over 1,000 years.

name: Rosebud Sioux
description: The Rosebud Sioux Tribe is a Lakota band in south-central South Dakota.

name: Sámi (Eastern)
description: The Eastern Sámi inhabit the Kola Peninsula of Russia and parts of Finland.

name: Sámi
description: The Sámi are indigenous to Sápmi (northern Scandinavia and the Kola Peninsula), Europe's only recognized indigenous people.

name: Saami - Inari
description: The Inari Sámi are indigenous to the Inari area of Finnish Lapland.

name: Saami - Kildin
description: The Kildin Sámi are indigenous to the Kola Peninsula of northwestern Russia.

name: Saami - Lule
description: The Lule Sámi are indigenous to the Lule River area in Norway and Sweden.

name: Saami - North
description: The North Sámi are the largest Sámi group, across northern Norway, Sweden, and Finland.

name: Saami - Skolt
description: The Skolt Sámi are indigenous to the border area of Finland, Norway, and Russia.

name: Saami - South
description: The South Sámi inhabit parts of central Norway and Sweden.

name: Salinan
description: The Salinan people are indigenous to the Salinas Valley in central California.

name: Salish-Kootenai
description: The Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes are indigenous to the Flathead Reservation in Montana.

name: Salt River Pima-Maricopa
description: The Salt River Pima-Maricopa Indian Community is near Scottsdale, Arizona.

name: Samburu
description: The Samburu are indigenous to north-central Kenya, closely related to the Maasai.

name: Samoan
description: The Samoan people are indigenous to the Samoan Islands in the South Pacific.

name: San Carlos Apache
description: The San Carlos Apache are indigenous to southeastern Arizona.

name: San - Hai//om
description: The Hai//om are a San people indigenous to the Etosha area of Namibia.

name: San - Ju/'hoansi
description: The Ju/'hoansi are a San people indigenous to the Kalahari Desert of Namibia and Botswana.

name: San - !Kung
description: The !Kung are a San people indigenous to the Kalahari Desert.

name: San
description: The San (Bushmen) are among the oldest indigenous peoples on Earth, indigenous to southern Africa.

name: Sandawe
description: The Sandawe are indigenous click-language speakers in Tanzania.

name: Sauk
description: The Sauk (Sac) people are indigenous to the upper Mississippi and Rock River area of Wisconsin and Illinois.

name: Secwepemc
description: The Secwepemc (Shuswap) people are indigenous to the south-central interior of British Columbia.

name: Selk'nam
description: The Selk'nam (Ona) were indigenous to Tierra del Fuego at the southern tip of South America.

name: Selkup
description: The Selkup are indigenous to the Ob River basin in western Siberia.

name: Seminole
description: The Seminole are indigenous to Florida, formed from various southeastern groups. Known for resistance to removal.

name: Seneca
description: The Seneca are the westernmost nation of the Haudenosaunee Confederacy, Keepers of the Western Door.

name: Sentinelese
description: The Sentinelese are indigenous to North Sentinel Island, one of the world's last uncontacted peoples.

name: Senufo
description: The Senufo people are indigenous to Ivory Coast, Mali, and Burkina Faso.

name: Seri
description: The Seri (Comcáac) people are indigenous to the coast of Sonora and Tiburón Island.

name: Serrano
description: The Serrano people are indigenous to the San Bernardino Mountains of southern California.

name: Shan
description: The Shan are indigenous to Shan State in eastern Myanmar, related to Thai peoples.

name: Shasta
description: The Shasta people are indigenous to the Shasta Valley and surrounding mountains in northern California.

name: Shawnee
description: The Shawnee people are indigenous to the Ohio Valley, known for leader Tecumseh's resistance movement.

name: Shipibo-Conibo
description: The Shipibo-Conibo are indigenous to the Ucayali River basin in the Peruvian Amazon.

name: Shoshone-Bannock
description: The Shoshone-Bannock Tribes reside on the Fort Hall Reservation in southeastern Idaho.

name: Shuar
description: The Shuar are indigenous to the Amazonian lowlands of Ecuador and Peru.

name: Siletz
description: The Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians include over 27 tribes from western Oregon.

name: Sisseton-Wahpeton
description: The Sisseton-Wahpeton Oyate are a Dakota people in the Lake Traverse Reservation of South Dakota.

name: Skokomish
description: The Skokomish people are indigenous to the Hood Canal area of Washington.

name: Snohomish
description: The Snohomish people are indigenous to the Snohomish River area of Washington.

name: Snoqualmie
description: The Snoqualmie people are indigenous to the Snoqualmie Valley in western Washington, known for Snoqualmie Falls.

name: Solomon Islanders
description: The indigenous peoples of the Solomon Islands are primarily Melanesian.

name: Somali
description: The Somali people are indigenous to the Horn of Africa, spanning Somalia, Ethiopia, Djibouti, and Kenya.

name: Sorbian
description: The Sorbs are a Slavic minority indigenous to Lusatia in eastern Germany.

name: Southern Cheyenne
description: The Southern Cheyenne are indigenous to the southern Great Plains of Colorado, Kansas, and Oklahoma.

name: Southern Paiute
description: The Southern Paiute are indigenous to the Colorado Plateau region spanning Utah, Arizona, Nevada, and California.

name: Spirit Lake
description: The Spirit Lake Tribe (Devils Lake Sioux) are a Dakota people in North Dakota.

name: Spokane
description: The Spokane Tribe is indigenous to the Spokane River area in eastern Washington.

name: Squaxin Island
description: The Squaxin Island Tribe are indigenous to the southern Puget Sound area of Washington.

name: Stó:lō
description: The Stó:lō people are indigenous to the Fraser Valley of British Columbia.

name: Standing Rock Sioux
description: The Standing Rock Sioux Tribe spans North and South Dakota, known for the 2016 pipeline protests.

name: Stillaguamish
description: The Stillaguamish Tribe inhabits the Stillaguamish River valley in western Washington.

name: Stockbridge-Munsee
description: The Stockbridge-Munsee are a Mohican people now in Wisconsin.

name: Stoney Nakoda
description: The Stoney Nakoda are indigenous to the Rocky Mountain foothills of Alberta.

name: Suquamish
description: The Suquamish people are indigenous to the Puget Sound region, particularly the area around present-day Bainbridge Island and the Kitsap Peninsula.

name: Swazi
description: The Swazi people are indigenous to Eswatini (Swaziland) and parts of South Africa.

name: Swinomish
description: The Swinomish people are indigenous to the Skagit River delta area in Washington.

name: Syilx
description: The Syilx (Okanagan) people are indigenous to the Okanagan Valley of British Columbia.

name: Taíno
description: The Taíno were the principal inhabitants of the Caribbean at European contact.

name: Tainui
description: Tainui is a confederation of Māori iwi in the Waikato region of New Zealand.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Amis
description: The Amis are the largest indigenous group in Taiwan, along the east coast.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Atayal
description: The Atayal are one of the largest indigenous groups of Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Bunun
description: The Bunun are indigenous to the central mountains of Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Paiwan
description: The Paiwan are indigenous to the southern mountains of Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Rukai
description: The Rukai are indigenous to the southern mountains of Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Saisiyat
description: The Saisiyat are one of the smallest indigenous groups in Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Truku
description: The Truku (Taroko) are indigenous to the Taroko Gorge area of eastern Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Tsou
description: The Tsou are indigenous to the Alishan area of central Taiwan.

name: Taiwan Indigenous - Yami
description: The Yami (Tao) are indigenous to Orchid Island off the southeastern coast of Taiwan.

name: Tarahumara
description: The Tarahumara (Rarámuri) are indigenous to the Copper Canyon region of Chihuahua, known as exceptional runners.

name: Tehuelche
description: The Tehuelche are indigenous to the Patagonian steppe of Argentina.

name: Terena
description: The Terena people are indigenous to Mato Grosso do Sul in western Brazil.

name: Tibetan
description: The Tibetan people are indigenous to the Tibetan Plateau, known for their Buddhist culture.

name: Ticuna
description: The Ticuna in Brazil are one of the most populous indigenous groups in the Brazilian Amazon.

name: Tikuna
description: The Tikuna are indigenous to the upper Amazon region where Brazil, Colombia, and Peru meet.

name: Tillamook
description: The Tillamook people are indigenous to the northern Oregon coast, known as skilled fishers and whalers.

name: Timucua
description: The Timucua were indigenous to northern and central Florida and southeastern Georgia.

name: Tlingit
description: The Tlingit people are indigenous to the Pacific Northwest coast of Alaska and British Columbia.

name: Toba
description: The Toba (Qom) people are indigenous to the Gran Chaco region of Argentina, Paraguay, and Bolivia.

name: Tohono O'odham
description: The Tohono O'odham are indigenous to the Sonoran Desert of southern Arizona and northern Mexico.

name: Tongan
description: The Tongan people are indigenous to the Kingdom of Tonga in the South Pacific.

name: Tongva
description: The Tongva (Gabrielino) people are indigenous to the Los Angeles Basin and the Southern Channel Islands.

name: Tonkawa
description: The Tonkawa people are indigenous to central Texas, known as skilled buffalo hunters.

name: Tonto Apache
description: The Tonto Apache are indigenous to the Tonto Basin in central Arizona.

name: Torres Strait Islanders
description: The Torres Strait Islanders are indigenous to the islands between Australia and Papua New Guinea.

name: Totonac
description: The Totonac are indigenous to eastern Mexico, Veracruz and Puebla states.

name: Toubou
description: The Toubou are indigenous to the Tibesti and Borkou regions of the central Sahara.

name: Trio
description: The Trio (Tiriyó) are indigenous to the border region of Suriname and Brazil.

name: Tsimshian
description: The Tsimshian people are indigenous to the northwestern coast of British Columbia.

name: Tsonga
description: The Tsonga (Shangaan) people are indigenous to southeastern Africa.

name: Tsuu T'ina
description: The Tsuu T'ina (Sarcee) are an Athabaskan-speaking people indigenous to the Calgary area of Alberta.

name: Tswana
description: The Tswana people are indigenous to Botswana and South Africa.

name: Tuareg
description: The Tuareg are indigenous to the Sahara Desert, known as the Blue People for their indigo-dyed clothing.

name: Tulalip
description: The Tulalip Tribes are a federally recognized confederation of tribes in western Washington.

name: Tunica
description: The Tunica people are indigenous to the lower Mississippi Valley, known as skilled traders.

name: Turkana
description: The Turkana are indigenous to the arid region around Lake Turkana in northwestern Kenya.

name: Turtle Mountain Chippewa
description: The Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa are indigenous to north-central North Dakota.

name: Tuscarora
description: The Tuscarora joined the Haudenosaunee as the sixth nation in 1722 after migrating from Carolina.

name: Tuvan
description: The Tuvan people are indigenous to the Tuva Republic in south-central Siberia, known for throat singing.

name: Tzeltal
description: The Tzeltal are a Maya people indigenous to the highlands of Chiapas, Mexico.

name: Tzotzil
description: The Tzotzil are a Maya people indigenous to the central highlands of Chiapas, Mexico.

name: Udege
description: The Udege are indigenous to the Sikhote-Alin Mountains of the Russian Far East.

name: Ulchi
description: The Ulchi are indigenous to the lower Amur River in the Russian Far East.

name: Umatilla
description: The Confederated Tribes of the Umatilla Indian Reservation include the Cayuse, Umatilla, and Walla Walla tribes.

name: Ute
description: The Ute people are indigenous to a large area of present-day Utah, Colorado, and New Mexico.

name: U'wa
description: The U'wa people are indigenous to the Sierra Nevada del Cocuy in Colombia.

name: Uyghur
description: The Uyghur are a Turkic people indigenous to the Xinjiang region of northwestern China.

name: Vanuatu ni-Vanuatu
description: The ni-Vanuatu are the indigenous Melanesian people of Vanuatu.

name: Vedda
description: The Vedda are the indigenous people of Sri Lanka, among the oldest inhabitants of the island.

name: Venda
description: The Venda people are indigenous to the Limpopo province of South Africa.

name: Vepsian
description: The Veps are a Finnic people indigenous to the area between Lakes Onega and Ladoga in Russia.

name: Wa
description: The Wa people are indigenous to the border region between Myanmar and China.

name: Waimiri-Atroari
description: The Waimiri-Atroari are indigenous to the area between Amazonas and Roraima states in Brazil.

name: Walla Walla
description: The Walla Walla people are indigenous to the Columbia Plateau of Oregon and Washington.

name: Wampanoag
description: The Wampanoag people are indigenous to southeastern Massachusetts and Rhode Island, known for their encounter with the Pilgrims.

name: Waorani
description: The Waorani people are indigenous to the Amazonian region of eastern Ecuador.

name: Wapishana
description: The Wapishana are indigenous to the border region of Guyana and Brazil.

name: Warao
description: The Warao people are indigenous to the Orinoco Delta of Venezuela.

name: Warm Springs
description: The Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs in central Oregon include the Wasco, Warm Springs, and Paiute peoples.
relatedTreaties: Treaty with Tribes of Middle Oregon (1855)

name: Washoe
description: The Washoe people are indigenous to the area around Lake Tahoe in the Sierra Nevada.

name: Wayana
description: The Wayana people are indigenous to the border region of Suriname, French Guiana, and Brazil.

name: Wayuu
description: The Wayuu are indigenous to the Guajira Peninsula spanning Colombia and Venezuela.

name: Wendat-Huron
description: The Wendat (Huron) people are indigenous to the area between Lake Simcoe and Georgian Bay in Ontario.

name: Western Shoshone
description: The Western Shoshone are indigenous to a vast area of the Great Basin, primarily in Nevada.

name: Wet'suwet'en
description: The Wet'suwet'en people are indigenous to the Bulkley River area of British Columbia.

name: White Earth Ojibwe
description: The White Earth Band of Ojibwe are in northwestern Minnesota.

name: White Mountain Apache
description: The White Mountain Apache are indigenous to the White Mountains of east-central Arizona.

name: Wichí
description: The Wichí people are indigenous to the Gran Chaco region of northern Argentina.

name: Wichita
description: The Wichita people are indigenous to the southern Plains, from Kansas to Texas, known as skilled farmers and traders.

name: Wind River Arapaho
description: The Northern Arapaho share the Wind River Reservation in Wyoming with the Eastern Shoshone.

name: Wintu
description: The Wintu people are indigenous to the upper Sacramento River valley in northern California.

name: Wiwa
description: The Wiwa are indigenous to the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta in Colombia.

name: Wiyot
description: The Wiyot people are indigenous to Humboldt Bay and the lower Eel River in northwestern California.

name: Wodaabe
description: The Wodaabe are a subgroup of the Fulani, known for the Gerewol beauty contest.

name: Wyandot
description: The Wyandot (Huron-Wendat) are an Iroquoian people indigenous to the Great Lakes region.

name: Xavante
description: The Xavante (A'uwẽ) are indigenous to the Mato Grosso state of central Brazil.

name: Xhosa
description: The Xhosa people are indigenous to the Eastern Cape of South Africa.

name: Yakama
description: The Yakama Nation is a confederation of 14 tribes in central Washington, with a large reservation near Yakima.

name: Yakut
description: The Yakut (Sakha) are indigenous to the Sakha Republic of northeastern Siberia.

name: Yana
description: The Yana people, including the Yahi, are indigenous to the area between the Sacramento Valley and the Cascade Range.

name: Yankton Sioux
description: The Yankton Sioux (Ihanktonwan) are a Nakota people in southeastern South Dakota.

name: Yanomami
description: The Yanomami are indigenous to the Amazon rainforest along the Brazil-Venezuela border, one of the largest isolated indigenous groups.

name: Yaqui
description: The Yaqui people are indigenous to the Sonoran Desert of northwestern Mexico.

name: Yavapai
description: The Yavapai people are indigenous to central Arizona.

name: Yi
description: The Yi (Nuosu) are indigenous to the mountains of Sichuan, Yunnan, and Guizhou in southwestern China.

name: Yuchi
description: The Yuchi people are indigenous to the Tennessee River valley in the southeastern US.

name: Yukpa
description: The Yukpa people are indigenous to the Sierra de Perijá mountains along the Colombia-Venezuela border.

name: Yupik
description: The Siberian Yupik are indigenous to the Chukchi Peninsula and St. Lawrence Island.

name: Yurok
description: The Yurok are the largest Native tribe in California, indigenous to the Klamath River area.

name: Zaghawa
description: The Zaghawa people are indigenous to the border region of Chad and Sudan.

name: Zapotec
description: The Zapotec are indigenous to the Oaxaca region of southern Mexico, builders of Monte Albán.

name: Zhuang
description: The Zhuang are the largest ethnic minority in China, indigenous to Guangxi.

name: Zulu
description: The Zulu are the largest ethnic group in South Africa, known for their warrior traditions.

name: Zuni
description: The Zuni people are indigenous to the Zuni River valley in western New Mexico.
